SHELL_PARSE_ERROR.description: zsh cannot parse command substitution or special characters
fixes: Use simple pipes instead of $(...)
Split into multiple commands
Use Read tool instead of cat/head

examples.good: ls -t *.jsonl | head -1  # then use Read tool

SYNTAX_ERROR.description: Python or shell syntax error in inline code
causes: f-strings with escaped quotes inside dict access
fixes: Extract dict values to variables before f-string
Write Python to a script file instead of -c
Use single quotes for outer string in python3 -c

COMMAND_NOT_FOUND.description: Command or binary doesn't exist or isn't in PATH
fixes: Check if installed: which <command>
Install if needed: brew install <package>
Use alternative (grep instead of rg)

examples.bad: rg pattern  # if ripgrep not installed
examples.good: grep -r pattern  # or: brew install ripgrep

PERMISSION_DENIED.description: No permission to execute or access file
fixes: Make script executable: chmod +x script.sh
Run with interpreter: python3 script.py
Check file ownership: ls -la file
Verify path exists: ls -la <parent_dir>
Check current directory: pwd
Create directory if needed: mkdir -p <dir>
Use absolute paths

NON_ZERO_EXIT.description: Command ran but returned non-zero exit code
fixes: Check stderr for details
For grep: exit 1 means no match (often ok)
Add || true if exit code doesn't matter

examples.bad: grep pattern file.txt  # exits 1 if no match

PIPE_ESCAPING.description: Pipe character incorrectly parsed in complex commands
causes: Pipe (|) in jq or complex expressions gets corrupted
Known Claude Code Bash tool issue with pipes in certain contexts
fixes: Use && to chain commands instead of pipes where possible
Write complex jq queries to a file, then run jq -f
Split piped commands into separate sequential Bash calls
Use process substitution <() instead of pipes when needed

examples.good: jq '.entries[] | .name' file.json  # or split into steps

USE_DEDICATED_TOOLS.description: Using bash for operations that have dedicated Claude tools
causes: Habit of using shell commands for file operations
Not aware of dedicated tools with better UX
fixes: Use Read tool instead of cat/head/tail for viewing files
Use Grep tool instead of grep/rg for searching content
Use Glob tool instead of find/ls for finding files
Use Edit tool instead of sed/awk for modifying files
Use Write tool instead of echo/cat heredoc for creating files

examples.good: Use Read tool with file_path and optional limit parameter

INTERACTIVE_COMMAND.description: Attempting to run interactive commands that require TTY
causes: Command requires user input or TTY
Command waits for password or confirmation
fixes: Use Edit/Write tools instead of vim/nano
Use Read tool instead of less/more
Use non-interactive flags: git commit -m instead of git commit
For password prompts, ask user to run command themselves

examples.good: Use Edit tool to modify file.txt

QUOTE_NESTING.description: Incorrect quote nesting in shell commands
causes: Mixing single and double quotes incorrectly
Trying to nest same quote type
JSON inside shell command needs careful escaping
fixes: Use heredoc (<<'EOF') for multi-line or complex content
Alternate quote types: outer single, inner double or vice versa
For JSON in commands, write to file first then reference
Use $'...' syntax for strings with special characters

TIMEOUT.description: Command killed after 2 minute default timeout (exit code 143)
causes: Long-running build, test, or analysis operations
BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS in shell env is ignored - only settings.json works
fixes: Configure timeout in ~/.claude/settings.json under env section
Set BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS: '1800000' for 30 min
Set BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS: '7200000' for 2 hour max
Restart Claude Code after changing settings.json
Or optimize command to complete faster

examples.bad: npm run build  # times out after 2 min

INTERMITTENT_ESCAPING.description: Sporadic escaping corruption (GitHub Issue #7387) - command displays correctly but executes with broken escaping
causes: Known intermittent bug in Claude Code bash tool (Issue #7387)
Dollar signs in $() get escaped as \$ ( with spaces
Occurs in 'spurts' - once triggered, tends to persist in session
fixes: IMMEDIATELY switch to script file approach when this occurs
Write command to temp.sh with heredoc: cat > temp.sh << 'EOF'
chmod +x temp.sh && ./temp.sh && rm temp.sh
Don't retry inline - the bug persists once triggered
Script files bypass Claude's escaping layer entirely

examples.bad: for f in $(find . -type f); do echo "$f"; done  # may corrupt to: for f in \$ ( find...
examples.good: Write to script.sh with heredoc, make executable, run

COMPLEX_INLINE.description: Moderately complex inline commands that should use script files
causes: Command contains pipes, substitution, loops, conditionals, or parameter expansion
These work in normal bash but fail in Claude Code's bash tool
Middle-ground complexity is where most failures occur
fixes: Write ALL complex commands to script files using heredoc
Pattern: cat > script.sh << 'EOF' (single-quoted EOF prevents expansion)
Then: chmod +x script.sh && ./script.sh
Optional cleanup: rm script.sh
Simple commands (ls, pwd, git status) are fine inline

examples.good:
cat > search.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
find . -name '*.py' | xargs grep 'pattern' | head -10
EOF
chmod +x search.sh && ./search.sh

AWS_TOKEN_EXPIRED.description: AWS SSO token has expired and needs re-authentication
causes: SSO session timed out (typically after 8-12 hours)
No profile specified and default not configured
fixes: Re-authenticate with SSO: aws sso login --profile <profile>
Available profiles: kare-dev-admin, kare-prod-admin (write access)
Read-only profiles: kare-dev-readonly, kare-prod-readonly (prefer for lookups)
Always specify --profile when running AWS commands

AWS_ACCESS_DENIED.description: AWS permissions error - insufficient IAM permissions
causes: Using readonly profile for write operations
IAM policy doesn't allow the action
fixes: For read operations: use kare-dev-readonly or kare-prod-readonly
For write operations: use kare-dev-admin or kare-prod-admin
Check which profile is needed for the operation
Verify the resource exists in the correct account

AWS_NOT_CONFIGURED.description: AWS CLI not configured or profile not found
fixes: Check available profiles: aws configure list-profiles
Use correct profile names: kare-dev-admin, kare-prod-admin, kare-dev-readonly, kare-prod-readonly
If AWS CLI not installed: brew install awscli
